thread.title: Glamorous British golfer calls for Donald Trump to became UK Prime Minister after performing US president-elect’s dance at tournament: ‘He’s better than Keir Starmer’
thread.title_full: Glamorous British golfer calls for Donald Trump to became UK Prime Minister after performing US president-elect’s dance at tournament: ‘He’s better than Keir Starmer’

author: James
title: Glamorous British golfer calls for Donald Trump to became UK Prime Minister after performing US president-elect’s dance at tournament: ‘He’s better than Keir Starmer’
text:
Trump is preparing for a second term as president after winning the elections in the United States He has won a number of high-profile fans, including several top athletes Charley Hull is the latest athlete to perform the 78-year-old’s iconic dance Follow DailyMail.com’s political live blog for all the latest news and updates By LEWIS BROWNING
Published: 20:54 GMT, November 22, 2024 | Updated: 21:56 GMT, November 22, 2024
A British golfer performed Donald Trump’s iconic dance at her latest tournament as she expressed her admiration for the newly elected US president.
Charley Hull, ranked 11th in the world, was competing in The Annika at Pelican Golf Club in Florida when she was caught performing the dance move that has become associated with the 78-year-old.
The 28-year-old Hull has long been tipped as a future star of the golf world. She played in several amateur tournaments in her early teens and was part of the European team that won the Solheim Cup in 2023.
She competed in the Curtis Cup – the amateur version of the Solheim Cup – when she was just 16, turning professional before her 17th birthday.
And while she is highly regarded for her skills on the course at the club when it comes to golf, it was her dance moves and tributes to the president-elect that saw her make headlines on Thursday.
During her speech after the event, she expressed her admiration for Trump – and even explained her wish that he could actually become prime minister.
Charley Hull brought out the Trump Dance in her final round fight with Nelly Korda on Sunday. This move is really taking over the sports world. pic.twitter.com/tCJGyPSvh3
— Mark Harris (@itismarkharris) November 18, 2024
British golfer Charley Hull has become the latest athlete to perform the iconic Donald Trump dance
Trump, who is preparing for a second term as president, danced during his campaign
Hull was caught performing the move while celebrating while playing in Florida in the United States
“I love Trump,” Hull said after shooting a 73 in the first round of the CME Group Tour Championship in Tiburon. ‘I think he’s brilliant.’
‘I like that he just says things, he doesn’t care what people think and he is straight to the point.
‘I wish he was head of Britain. I just like him as a person. He is slightly better than our Prime Minister.’
Trump won a second term as president of the United States earlier this month when he defeated Kamala Harris in the next term’s election.
He has won a number of fans since the start of his campaign, while other sports stars such as American UFC star Jon Jones and USMNT footballer Christian Pulisic have also performed the dance in recent weeks.
Fellow golfer Bryson DeChambeau recently featured Trump on his YouTube channel and joined him on stage during his victory rally.
Rory McIlroy, who has golfed with Trump in the past, has suggested that his return to the White House could bridge the gap between the PGA Tour and the LIV group thanks to the “smartest man in the world”, Elon Musk, working closely . with him.
Hull, wearing sunglasses and a white hat, turned to someone apparently standing on the course before holding out her arms in typical Trump fashion. She added that it would be “cool” to play a round of golf with the “legend” Trump.
She showed her admiration for Trump and claimed he is better than Prime Minister Kier Starmer
Hull explained that she likes that he “doesn’t care what people think and he gets straight to the point.”
On the prospect of a round of golf with “legend” Trump, Hull said, “That would be cool.”
Trump is an avid golf fanatic and owns a number of courses around the world, including Turnberry in Scotland, which is ranked as the third best golf course in Britain and Ireland.
He has played golf with the likes of Tiger Woods in the past, and could soon be back on the course with some big names as he prepares for another term.
The businessman performed his dance at a sporting event last weekend, when he was in the crowd at UFC 309 to watch Jones defeat Stipe Miocic at Madison Square Garden.
It is expected that he will not be in Las Vegas this weekend for the Formula 1 Grand Prix.
Share or comment on this article: Glamorous British golfer calls on Donald Trump to become Prime Minister of Great Britain after performing the American president-elect’s dance during a tournament: ‘He’s better than Keir Starmer’
